title: I Went To Thank Her,
content:
I went to thank her,
    But she slept;
    Her bed a funnelled stone,
    With nosegays at the head and foot,
    That travellers had thrown,

    Who went to thank her;
    But she slept.
    'T was short to cross the sea
    To look upon her like, alive,
    But turning back 't was slow.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "I Went To Thank Her,", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
